title: hieracium murorum subsp. umbelliferum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. umbelliferum, also known as Hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and jagged leaves. Traditionally, Hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and reducing inflammation due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Hawkweed is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the leaves and flowers being harvested for their medicinal properties.
